model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Florida holds a commanding ~5.7-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Vanderbilt (KenPom #4, AdjEM +33.83 vs. KenPom #12, AdjEM +28.12), anchored by the nation's 5th-best defense (AdjDE 91.8) that presents the toughest defensive assignment Vanderbilt's elite offense (6th nationally, AdjOE 127.3) has faced all season. While Vanderbilt's stunning 91-74 SEC Tournament demolition of Florida is a legitimate data point, Jay Bilas and expert consensus explicitly noted that Florida will be better prepared in a single-elimination NCAA setting with full preparation time, and the Commodores' defense ranked only 28th nationally (AdjDE 99.2) is a severe liability against Florida's top-9 offense. Florida's elite two-way profile, superior efficiency margin, and reigning national champion pedigree under Todd Golden give them a clear edge, even as Vanderbilt's offensive firepower and proven head-to-head ability to beat this specific opponent keep confidence at a moderate level.

reasoningStep.title: Florida vs. Vanderbilt Sweet 16 — South Region
reasoningStep.summary: Florida is the analytically superior team by ~5.7 KenPom points and ~6 Torvik points, with an elite two-way profile (top-5 defense, top-9 offense) that outclasses Vanderbilt's offense-heavy identity (top-6 offense, 28th-ranked defense). The SEC Tournament upset by Vanderbilt is a real concern but is discounted by expert consensus and the full-preparation NCAA context. Florida's superior efficiency margin, elite defensive identity, and reigning champion pedigree make them the clear pick, though Vanderbilt's elite offense and head-to-head history cap confidence at a moderate level.
evidence: Florida KenPom #4 (AdjEM +33.83, AdjO 125.6 [9th], AdjD 91.8 [5th]); Torvik #4 (AdjOE 126.1 [11th], AdjDE 92.2 [5th]). Vanderbilt KenPom #12 (AdjEM +28.12, AdjO 127.3 [6th], AdjD 99.2 [28th]); Torvik #10 (AdjOE 127.8 [6th], AdjDE 99.2 [28th]). Efficiency gap: ~5.7 KenPom points in Florida's favor.

Jay Bilas picked Vanderbilt to reach the Sweet 16 but explicitly stated he does NOT believe the Commodores will repeat what they did to Florida in the SEC Tournament, signaling expert consensus favoring Florida in the NCAA context.

In the SEC Tournament semifinal (March 14), Florida was a 7.5–8.5 point favorite; Vanderbilt won outright 91-74. For the NCAA Sweet 16, Florida is again favored, reflecting the market's view that the efficiency gap is real despite the head-to-head result.

Vanderbilt's defense ranked only 28th nationally (AdjDE 99.2) is a severe liability against Florida's top-9 offense — the critical matchup asymmetry that distinguishes this game from the SEC Tournament context.
Both teams arrive healthy with no significant injury concerns reported, making the efficiency gap and matchup dynamics the decisive factors in Florida's favor.

modelTrace.content: {"pick":{"gameId":"s16-south-1","winnerId":"florida","confidence":0.72,"rationale":"Florida holds a commanding ~5.7-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over Vanderbilt (KenPom #4, AdjEM +33.83 vs. KenPom #12, AdjEM +28.12), anchored by the nation's 5th-best defense (AdjDE 91.8) that presents the toughest defensive assignment Vanderbilt's elite offense (6th nationally, AdjOE 127.3) has faced all season. While Vanderbilt's stunning 91-74 SEC Tournament demolition of Florida is a legitimate data point, Jay Bilas and expert consensus explicitly noted that Florida will be better prepared in a single-elimination NCAA setting with full preparation time, and the Commodores' defense ranked only 28th nationally (AdjDE 99.2) is a severe liability against Florida's top-9 offense. Florida's elite two-way profile, superior efficiency margin, and reigning national champion pedigree under Todd Golden give them a clear edge, even as Vanderbilt's offensive firepower and proven head-to-head ability to beat this specific opponent keep confidence at a moderate level."},"reasoningStep":{"id":"s16-south-1-analysis","title":"Florida vs. Vanderbilt Sweet 16 — South Region","summary":"Florida is the analytically superior team by ~5.7 KenPom points and ~6 Torvik points, with an elite two-way profile (top-5 defense, top-9 offense) that outclasses Vanderbilt's offense-heavy identity (top-6 offense, 28th-ranked defense). The SEC Tournament upset by Vanderbilt is a real concern but is discounted by expert consensus and the full-preparation NCAA context. Florida's superior efficiency margin, elite defensive identity, and reigning champion pedigree make them the clear pick, though Vanderbilt's elite offense and head-to-head history cap confidence at a moderate level.","evidence":["Florida KenPom #4 (AdjEM +33.83, AdjO 125.6 [9th], AdjD 91.8 [5th]); Torvik #4 (AdjOE 126.1 [11th], AdjDE 92.2 [5th]). Vanderbilt KenPom #12 (AdjEM +28.12, AdjO 127.3 [6th], AdjD 99.2 [28th]); Torvik #10 (AdjOE 127.8 [6th], AdjDE 99.2 [28th]). Efficiency gap: ~5.7 KenPom points in Florida's favor.","Jay Bilas picked Vanderbilt to reach the Sweet 16 but explicitly stated he does NOT believe the Commodores will repeat what they did to Florida in the SEC Tournament, signaling expert consensus favoring Florida in the NCAA context.","In the SEC Tournament semifinal (March 14), Florida was a 7.5–8.5 point favorite; Vanderbilt won outright 91-74. For the NCAA Sweet 16, Florida is again favored, reflecting the market's view that the efficiency gap is real despite the head-to-head result.","Vanderbilt's defense ranked only 28th nationally (AdjDE 99.2) is a severe liability against Florida's top-9 offense — the critical matchup asymmetry that distinguishes this game from the SEC Tournament context.","Both teams arrive healthy with no significant injury concerns reported, making the efficiency gap and matchup dynamics the decisive factors in Florida's favor."]}}
